readerContext.desiredUpgrade: Understand where leverage moves when core capabilities commoditize.

hero.title: Workflow ownership is becoming the real AI moat.
hero.lede: A compact strategic read on how distribution, context, and interface control are capturing more value as raw model access spreads.
hero.signal: AI capability is diffusing faster than durable workflow ownership.
hero.thesis: As model quality converges, margin and defensibility migrate toward whoever owns recurring workflow and context.
hero.tension: The market still talks about models, but value capture is moving up the stack.
hero.promise: Read this to understand where power is actually shifting and how to position against it.

topLine.title: Distribution is becoming as important as the model.
topLine.body: Advantage no longer lives only in training or accessing better models. It increasingly lives in controlling interface, workflow, user context, and distribution. Whoever packages intelligence best captures the user relationship and, with it, the margin.
topLine.stakes: This changes how to evaluate products, careers, and moats in the AI stack.

items.label: Models
items.text: Raw model differentiation is narrowing; workflow control matters more.

items.label: Product
items.text: Integrated suites are gaining ground against standalone tools.

items.label: Counter-signal
items.text: In narrow, high-stakes workflows, frontier performance still changes buying decisions.

items.label: Work
items.text: System orchestration matters more than proficiency with a single model.

items.title: 1. The battleground is moving
items.body: As models commoditize, competition shifts toward product integration, workflow control, and usage frequency.
items.claim: Integrated distribution compresses standalone feature advantage faster than many teams expect.
items.explanation: Incumbents can fold AI into surfaces they already own, reducing the value of isolated feature superiority.
items.implication: Products need workflow depth, not just model access.

items.title: 2. Value is concentrating in context
items.body: The scarce asset is no longer inference alone. It is structured context: memory, proprietary data, user habits, and embedding inside real operating systems for work.
items.claim: Persistent context becomes harder to replace than raw inference.
items.explanation: Systems that accumulate non-portable usage context gain switching cost over time.

items.title: 3. Interface determines capture
items.body: Interface is not cosmetic. It shapes what gets measured, repeated, remembered, and monetized.
items.claim: The working surface often decides who captures the economic relationship.
items.explanation: The layer where work actually happens governs repetition, context accumulation, and monetization rights.
items.implication: Owning the daily surface can matter more than owning the core model.

items.label: Power gaining
items.text: Platforms with distribution, data, and daily user surface area.
items.powerShift: Leverage compounds where workflow and context accumulate.

items.label: Power losing
items.text: Vendors whose moat depended mainly on access to a better model.
items.powerShift: Feature-level differentiation erodes when access broadens.

items.label: Opportunity
items.text: Vertical products that turn AI into concrete workflow.
items.powerShift: Specialization wins when it embeds into recurring behavior.

items.headline: Build where context compounds
items.body: Skills tied to orchestration, memory, and workflow embedding gain leverage as model access broadens.

items.headline: Feature AI is fragile
items.body: If the wedge is only model access, bundling pressure will arrive faster than expected.

items.headline: Watch where dependency forms
items.body: Expect durable vendor power where workflow, compliance, and context become hard to unwind.

reusableLesson.title: Reusable lesson
reusableLesson.pattern: When a base capability commoditizes, value often migrates to the layer that owns workflow, distribution, or switching cost.
applyWhen: A new capability becomes broadly accessible
Incumbents can bundle it into existing channels
Users create non-portable context over repeated use
reusableLesson.takeaway: Do not evaluate advantage only at the capability layer; check where repetition, context, and distribution accumulate.

items.text: Do enterprise suites keep bundling AI into default workflow surfaces?
Do standalone model leaders retain pricing power despite broad access?
Does retention correlate more with context depth than raw model quality?
Which products turn occasional use into operational dependency?
